name: Pureed Vegetable Soup with Broccoli Florets
recipeInstructions:
1. In a Dutch oven or 4-5 quart saucepan, combine the potatoes, celery, carrots, onion, garlic, stock, and seasonings. Bring to a boil, cover, lower heat, and simmer until the vegetables are very tender (about 10 minutes). The potatoes must be fully cooked to thicken the soup properly.
2. While the soup is simmering, steam the broccoli florets.
3. When the simmered vegetables are tender, transfer them and the broth to a food processor and process until smooth.
4. Return the pureed soup mixture to the pan. Stir in the broccoli florets.
5. In a measuring cup, combine the remaining ingredients (egg yolk, soy sauce). Add to the soup and heat; do not allow the mixture to boil.
6. Top each serving with garnish if desired. Variations: Substitute 2 cups steamed chopped carrots or 2 cups steamed peas for the broccoli. Use 1 tablespoon curry powder instead of thyme and nutmeg. Garnish with thin apple slices or raisins. Add 1 cup shredded Cheddar cheese; melt into the soup base before adding the broccoli. Alternatively, use a pinch of dry mustard instead of nutmeg. If you prefer a thinner soup, add more milk, vegetable stock, or water.
